reasoning: The 2026 special election in Ohio features GOP incumbent Jon Husted battling Democratic former Senator Sherrod Brown. Ohio leans solidly Republican (Trump +11 in 2024), establishing a strong base-rate disadvantage for Democrats. However, four massive factors counterbalance this and point to a slight Democratic edge: 1) The 2026 fundamentals are likely to favor Democrats, as midterms traditionally punish the incumbent president's party (Trump). 2) Jon Husted is seriously hindered by the FirstEnergy utility bribery scandal; he was identified in court documents as "State Official 2" and testified as a defense witness at the criminal trial in March 2026, dealing a severe blow to his reputation. 3) Polling tracks this deterioration: Husted held a 6-point lead in August 2025, but a March 2026 poll by a GOP firm showed Brown leading him by 2 points. 4) The betting market reflects this precise shift, advancing the Democrat's chances from 40% in early February to 60.5% in mid-April. Counterbalancing this is a monumental $79M independent expenditure campaign from the Senate Leadership Fund signaling that Republicans will vehemently defend the seat. Overall, the structural environment and Husted's scandal currently make Brown a slight favorite. Probabilities regress slightly toward 0.5 to reflect the "N/A" dummy resolution dates in the prompt.
